title: Dortmanna zeylanica
intro: Dortmanna zeylanica, also known as Black Borneo, is a plant species belonging to the family Rutaceae, commonly found in tropical regions of Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, which can help alleviate various health issues such as fever, digestive problems, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Dortmanna zeylanica include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Dortmanna zeylanica is classified as a species of plant in the family Lythraceae. It belongs to the genus Dortmanna, which is a group of flowering plants. The plant is native to Sri Lanka. The classification of Dortmanna zeylanica is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, and the phylum Magnoliophyta. This classification places it within the broader category of angiosperms.
